nodes.en: Traditional art techniques are craft methods and aesthetic practices maintained across generations—weaving, metalwork, carving, painting. They preserve manual skill and aesthetic sensibility specific to cultural contexts.
nodes.cs: Tradiční umělecké techniky jsou řemeslnické metody a estetické praktiky udržované generacemi—tkalcovství, zpracování kovu, řezba, malba. Zachovávají manuální dovednost a estetickou sensibilitu specifickou pro kulturní kontexty.
nodes.brief:
Traditional art techniques are craft methods and aesthetic practices maintained across generations — weaving, metalwork, carving, painting — preserving manual skill and a cultural context's aesthetic sensibility rather than any single finished object.

The branch is the hands-on counterpart to [[10.06]] Stories & Narratives, which a technique frequently illustrates or accompanies (a woven narrative textile, a carved story panel), and it borders [[06]] Arts & Creative Expression wherever the same technique is practiced as contemporary fine art rather than inherited craft — the dividing line is transmission lineage, not the object itself. It sits alongside [[10.03]] Traditions & Customs, whose material culture a technique often produces, under the shared parent [[10]] Cultural Preservation.

The [UNESCO Traditional Craftsmanship](https://ich.unesco.org/en/traditional-craftsmanship-00057) domain is the standard reference for safeguarding these techniques, and the [World Crafts Council](https://www.worldcrafts.org/) documents living craft traditions by region.
